title: McCarthy rejected for House speaker with GOP in disarray
Groups urge GOP House leaders to condemn political violence
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dozens of military veterans on Wednesday hand-delivered letters to top Republicans in the U.S. House, calling on them to publicly condemn political violence as the second anniversary of the Jan.

title: Biden intends to make his first visit to US-Mexico border
shortDescription: HEBRON, Ky. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday he intends to visit the U.S.-Mexico border — his first since taking office — in connection with his meeting next week in Mexico City with the leaders of Mexico and Canada.

title: Biden highlights bipartisanship during House GOP chaos
shortDescription: COVINGTON, Kentucky (AP) — President Joe Biden on Wednesday held out the promised makeover of a dilapidated bridge over the Ohio River as a symbol of what can happen when Republicans and Democrats work together — even as he condemned what he labeled an “embarrassing” scene of GOP disarray back in Washington.

title: Proud Boys go to trial on sedition as Jan. 6 probe heats up
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — As members of the Proud Boys extremist group stormed past police lines and swarmed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, their leader cheered them on from afar, prosecutors say.

title: Against McCarthy: Hard-right members stage GOP rebellion
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Republicans opposing Rep. Kevin McCarthy's bid to become the next House speaker generally have made their mark as part of the GOP's ultra-conservative wing, eager to pick a fight with leaders from both parties and rarely finding major bipartisan bills to be a good thing.

title: EXPLAINER: What to expect on Day 2 of House speaker election
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — What was expected to be a day of triumph for House Republicans coming into the majority turned into chaos Tuesday as interparty fighting over who should lead them in their new reign ended with no speaker in the rostrum.

title: Rep.-elect Santos quiet as speaker vote delays swearing-in
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — Republican Rep.-elect George Santos of New York, who is accused of lying about large swaths of his background and accomplishments, will have to wait to add one thing with certainty to his resume: U.S.

title: McConnell celebrates milestone as Senate Dems retain power
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are again claiming the Senate majority, but much of the chamber’s focus Tuesday was on the top Republican as Mitch McConnell became the longest serving Senate leader in history.

title: Serbian president rejects calls for sanctions against Russia
shortDescription: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia’s president said Wednesday that the European Union's calls for his country to join sanctions against Russia over the war in Ukraine represent “a brutal” interference in the internal affairs of the Balkan state, which has asked to join the EU.

title: Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont sworn into office, seeks tax cut
shortDescription: Sworn into office for a second four-year term, Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont on Wednesday called on state legislators to pass a "meaningful middle class tax cut," saying it's time for the state to refocus on economic growth and opportunity now that the emergency of the pandemic is fading and years of state fiscal crises have ended.

title: Massachusetts GOP Gov. Baker marks final full day in office
shortDescription: BOSTON (AP) — Massachusetts Gov. Charlie Baker engaged in a series of traditional ceremonies during his last full day as governor on Wednesday, marking the transition of power in the top office on Beacon Hill from Republican to Democratic hands.

title: GOP lawmaker seated despite NYC residency questions by Dems
shortDescription: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — A new Republican New York state Assemblyman took his seat in the chamber Wednesday despite the threat of being blocked by Democrats who question whether he meets residency requirements.

title: New Hampshire Legislature gets down to work
shortDescription: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — New Hampshire lawmakers debated rules changes that reflected the dramatically divided House on Wednesday as the state's new Legislature began its first official day of work.The House convened with Republicans holding a razor thin 201-197 majority, with two seats vacant.

title: Newly empowered Minnesota Democrats pledge to move swiftly
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Minnesota's Democratic leaders pledged Wednesday to use their new control of the state Legislature — and a $17.6 billion budget surplus — to improve the economic security of residents and to quickly enact protections for abortion rights.

title: US moves to reopen Solomon Islands embassy to counter China
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is plowing ahead with plans to re-open the U.S. embassy in the Solomon Islands in a bid to counter China’s increasing assertiveness in the Pacific.The State Department has informed Congress that it will establish soon an interim embassy in the Solomons’ capital of Honiara on the site of a former U.S.

title: Russia’s hypersonic missile-armed ship to patrol global seas
shortDescription: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday sent a frigate armed with the country's latest Zircon hypersonic missile on a trans-ocean cruise in a show of force as tensions with the West escalate over the war in Ukraine.

title: Missouri lawmakers open session focused on ballot measures
shortDescription: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Missouri lawmakers on Wednesday returned to work for a session that Republican leader have said will focus on making it harder to amend Missouri's Constitution. GOP lawmakers have been trying for years to crack down on ballot initiatives, which have been used to enact policies that the Republican-led Legislature either avoided dealing with or opposed.

title: State senator embarrassed at public intoxication arrest
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A state senator in South Carolina charged with public intoxication after being found in his parked SUV in a strip mall lot said he is embarrassed and that law enforcement acted properly taking him to jail for several hours.

title: White House gun violence program with philanthropies ends
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — It was small, as Washington celebrations go — two senior Biden administration advisers gathered with program participants near the White House on a Thursday afternoon in December to mark the end of a little-known initiative with a budget of less than $8 million.

title: Myanmar army leader touts election plan on Independence Day
shortDescription: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar’s ruling military leader pardoned over 7,000 prisoners, including some political detainees, and detailed plans for an election later this year during a ceremony Wednesday marking the 75th anniversary of independence from Britain.

title: Beshear to welcome Biden, give State of Commonwealth speech
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Gov. Andy Beshear will welcome President Joe Biden to Kentucky and join the president as he speaks in Covington before returning to Frankfort to give his State of the Commonwealth address.

title: President Biden says it's his 'intention' to visit the U.S.-Mexico border
shortDescription: President Biden says it's his 'intention' to visit the U.S.-Mexico border.

title: Biden and McConnell appear together, striking contrast to Kevin McCarthy House chaos
shortDescription: McConnell hailed a $1.6 billion bridge project for Kentucky and Ohio a legislative miracle.' Biden said it proves "we can work together."

title: 'Groundhog Day': House speaker vote goes to sixth ballot amid GOP chaos
shortDescription: Republican Rep. Kevin McCarthy lost a fifth vote for speaker of the House, as GOP opposition blocked him on the second day of Congress.

title: What happens after McCarthy loses speaker votes? The process explained
shortDescription: Candidates for speaker are nominated from the floor by members of their parties. The speaker who is chosen does not have to be a member of Congress.

title: Biden praises McConnell for bipartisan work
shortDescription: President Joe Biden praised Minority Leader Mitch McConnell for his bipartisan cooperation to pass infrastructure legislation in Covington, Kentucky.

title: Why DeSantis and Republicans have declared war on corporate America
shortDescription: Republicans like Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis are waging war against "corporate wokeness" from CRT to ESG to score political points with their base.

title: FBI ups reward in Capitol pipe bomb probe to $500K
shortDescription: Pipe bombs were planted near the Republican and Democratic national committee headquarters the night before the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack.

title: New Hampshire Democrats to DNC: Please don't punish us in 2024 primary
shortDescription: New Hampshire Democrats say they have no ability to change state election laws to comply with the DNC's 2024 presidential nominating calendar mandate.

title: Meet Hakeem Jeffries, Pelosi's successor for Democratic leadership
shortDescription: House Democrats voted to elect Hakeem Jeffries as leader. Here's what you should know about the representative from NY's 8th congressional district.

title: Kevin McCarthy fails to win votes for speaker
shortDescription: GOP leader Kevin McCarthy failed to win 218 votes to become speaker of the House after three rounds of voting.

title: GOP rebels reject McCarthy as House adjourns without a speaker: recap
shortDescription: GOP turmoil sank Republican Rep. Kevin McCarthy on three votes for speaker of the House in the new Congress. Dem. Rep. Hakeem Jeffries also got votes.

title: OnPolitics: House GOP fails to elect new leader in historic first
shortDescription: Not since 1923 has the vote for a new speaker needed more than one ballot. Here's what's next.

title: After George Santos is sworn into Congress, could he be impeached?
shortDescription: George Santos, a newly elected New York Republican congressman, admitted last week to lying to voters about his life. What can Congress do about it?

title: Notable lawmakers to know: Who will be leading Congress in 2023?
shortDescription: It's the first day of the 118th Congress which means a fresh set of senators and representatives are being seated. Here are the lawmakers to watch.

shortDescription: Rep. Kevin McCarthy is facing challenges from other Republicans in his bid to be elected speaker of the House.

title: Who are the Republicans balking at Kevin McCarthy as a House speaker?
shortDescription: There are two main groups of GOP lawmakers who could stand in Kevin McCarthy's way of becoming the next House speaker when the new Congress is seated.

title: How a new Congress will change the direction of the country: What we know
shortDescription: House GOP Leader Kevin McCarthy said having Republicans in the majority will change the direction of the country. The new Congress is sworn in Jan. 3.

title: Culture wars: House Republicans attack Pentagon for 'woke' policies
shortDescription: House Republicans plan to open a new front in the culture wars: attacking the Pentagon for its "woke" social policies.

title: As GOP takes the House, a blockade for Biden's congressional agenda
shortDescription: President Joe Biden’s agenda will largely be blunted in a divided Congress, with GOP lawmakers pledging to block Democrats' legislative priorities.

title: Trump hosts New Year's Eve party, ending year with legal, political setbacks
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump had some legal and political notable setbacks in 2022. He ended it with his annual New Year's Eve party at Mar-a-Lago.

title: Week one of the new Congress: Picking a speaker, investigations, more
shortDescription: The 118th Congress is due to be seated on Tuesday and lawmakers have some housekeeping before they can get to business. Here's what to keep an eye on.

title: State AG: Ex-Trump chief of staff Meadows won’t face NC voter fraud charges
shortDescription: Mark Meadows, former White House chief of staff to Trump, won’t face voter fraud charges for his 2020 registration and absentee vote, N.C. AG said.

title: What happened this year in politics? Midterm elections, Roe overturned
shortDescription: In a busy year for politics, the midterm elections shared the stage with the Supreme Court, ex-President Trump's legal troubles and more during 2022.

title: OnPolitics special edition: Trump's tax returns released
shortDescription: Nearly 6,000 pages include personal returns, business returns.

title: Top takeaways from Donald Trump's tax returns
shortDescription: Six years of former President Donald Trump's tax returns have been released by Congress after an eight-year legal battle.

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The IRS Hasn’t Released Nearly Half a Million Nonprofit Tax Records
shortDescription: If you’re trying to evaluate a charity this year, you might have a hard time. The IRS is behind on releasing Form 990s, limiting access to key financial information the public uses to evaluate the nation’s tax-exempt companies.

title: Trump, Mum as McCarthy Flailed, Calls on Republicans to Embrace Him for Speaker.
shortDescription: Trump made a direct appeal to House Republicans on Wednesday to support McCarthy on his on his website.By Annie Karni

title: McCarthy Scrounges for Votes on 2nd Day of Speaker Fight
shortDescription: The House continued a historic floor showdown — the first in a century — prompted by the Republican leader’s failure to secure a majority to become speaker.By Catie Edmondson

title: How Far Right Are the 20 Republicans Who Voted Against McCarthy
shortDescription: Most denied the 2020 election, are members of the ultraconservative Freedom Caucus, or both.By Danielle Ivory, Charlie Smart and Karen Yourish

title: Hakeem Jeffries Takes Over as Leader of House Democrats
